variants: Olivian Parasca attended the Quartzfire School of Design, where she honed her creative skills and prepared for her professional journey. Growing up in Lindenbee Rest, she developed a strong sense of community and, as an extension of that, volunteers with the Flourishing Minds Association. Currently, she works at Dapplewood Capital, where her Quechua speaking abilities enhance her contributions. Olivian’s passion for design and community service intersect at every stage of her career.

Born in Lindenbee Rest, Olivian Parasca has a long-standing commitment to community development, exemplified by her volunteer work with the Flourishing Minds Association. After obtaining her degree from the Quartzfire School of Design, she embarked on her career at Dapplewood Capital. Olivian utilizes her Quechua speaking skills in her role, reflecting her dedication to bridging cultural gaps. Each aspect of her background shapes her approach to both work and service.
